plan.reasoning: SMILES Consistency Check: The SMILES [NH2] represents NH2•, a radical with one heavy atom (N at index 0, SP3 hybridization, radical_electrons: 1). This matches a reactive species for adsorption via the radical center. No hybridization mismatch. Radical check: Binding atom index 0 has radical_electrons > 0, suitable for ReactiveSpecies. Analyze Old Plans: History is None, so no previous attempts. This is the initial plan. Formulate New Plan: Goal is to find the lowest energy adsorption configuration. Start with the simplest and most likely site for NH2, which typically binds via N in an ontop position on metal surfaces. Surface has ontop sites on Re. Propose ontop adsorption via N (index 0) on Re. Since termination is disabled but hard limit is 1, propose this as the initial plan. Analyze Request: User wants the lowest-energy configuration for [NH2] on the surface. Analyze Adsorbate: Single N atom with radical, suitable for monodentate binding via index 0. Analyze Surface: Ontop sites available on Re. Formulate Plan: Use ReactiveSpecies for radical, ontop site with surface_binding_atoms ['Re'], adsorbate_binding_indices [0].
